title: B4. Hensel Lifting Verification
label: live_repo_baseline_execute
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B4-hensel-lifting-verification
Title: B4. Hensel Lifting Verification

Question:
Find least prime p where there exists n such that n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).
Then find least m where m^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2).

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: p=5, m=13
JUSTIFICATION: The equation n^4 + 1 ≡ 0 (mod p^2) requires that n^4 ≡ -1 (mod p^2). First, find the least prime p for which there exists n such that n^4 ≡ -1 (mod p). Checking small primes, p=5 satisfies n^4 ≡ -1 (mod 5) because 2^4 = 16 ≡ 1 (mod 5), but 3^4 = 81 ≡ 1 (mod 5), so no n satisfies n^4 ≡ -1 (mod 5). Next prime 13: 5^4 = 625 ≡ 1 (mod 13), 2^4=16 ≡ 3 (mod 13), 3^4
